title: How To Inject The Cheeks. Cheek Injection Points Tutorial.
filename: How To Inject The Cheeks. Cheek Injection Points Tutorial..en.vtt
excerpt: So let's start with this injection Point here so this is on the lateral zygoma but it's on the upper surface of that zygoma now look if you look at the angle of this needle the way I've placed it is slightly pointing upwards that that's because in my mind's eye it is placed just …

segments.text: so let's start with this injection Point here so this is on the lateral zygoma but it's on the upper surface of that zygoma now look if you look at the angle of this needle the way I've placed it is slightly pointing upwards that that's because in my mind's eye it is placed just here and this surface if you go at 90° to it is ever so slightly pointing upwards which is great because that's what we want to do with cheeks so lateral injection onto the periosteum and you can Pate this with your needle you can actually feel the curve of this particular when you're sliding off inferiorly when you're at this angle you can sense when it's a 90° angle and you can feel when you're going off in the wrong plane so little Taps on the lateral surface of the lateral zygoma for a defining injection now there are other injections you can do more laterally if you need to later on but these are the core injections we're covering today the next injection point is the angle of the zygoma so this is the greatest curvature on the bone it's usually not the greatest curvature on the face because as we get old ER the

curve shifts down and medial so you end up with a cheek with the greatest curvature is here the remedy for it is to add volume here and here to counterbalance the excess volume medially and this injection is key so you would slide your needle down onto the periostium after feeling the shape of the bone so before you even do inject I always recommend feeling anterior surface lateral surface and then with your fingers work your way up until you feel the Apex we can then choose an entry point that will allow your needle to land directly on the Apex which you can usually feel and this is where you can inject a Bolis that would give a beautifying injection the next injection is the lateral lid cheek Junction this is a powerful injection for restoration but it has that risk of overtreatment so here we often want to use a thinner product so you might use an intermediate grade thickness product and I've also shifted my angle of injection slightly in recent years I now prefer to go in with the bevel facing down touch the par oium and then inject because it's so

in recent years I now prefer to go in
with the bevel facing down touch the par
oium and then inject because it's so

easy for product to unfortunately come up to the surface then you get left with a little bump or an irregularity so this is a new tweak that I've made recently to try and reduce the risk of that happening is to go in bevel down into the same position touching the periosteum one injection and then mold it with your finger the next entry point is this anterior injection so this I believe does require a 90° angle of injection so that you are entering the anterior face as soon as you enter from a different angle you risk touching different parts of the periosteum and not projecting the cheek in the way that we want so anterior cheek projection requires a 90° angle of injection straight down touch the periosteum uh and then a little bolus after aspirating and you should see the cheek anteriorly project another really useful thing about this injection is sometimes when you can see the zygomatic ligament and it's depleted above the zygomatic ligament you end up with a cheek Shadow which you can completely remedy with this injection you must of course differentiate it from M edema causing a shadow which is the opposite way around

finally our last injection is this one here as I said I don't actually enter like this because I think we're in direct path of the transverse facial artery in some people so this is an injection point where the bolus is underneath the area I showed you but I usually enter like this and stay in the hypodermis little depth check aspirate and then do a little bolus while molding the product up afterwards so you end up with a rounded cheek still with the High Point defining the cheek here and here if you're interested in learning much more about anatomy and the techniques that I use make sure you sign up to my new movement it's called Pro injector click on the link it's not open yet but it will be soon [Music]
